chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Prohibition of Hunting in the Sanctuary and Its Rulings

text: 2360 - وَ رَوَى حَرِيزٌ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ أُهْدِيَ لَهُ حَمَامٌ أَهْلِيٌّ وَ جِيءَ بِهِ وَ هُوَ فِي اَلْحَرَمِ مُحِلٌّ قَالَ «إِنْ أَصَابَ مِنْهُ شَيْئاً فَلْيَتَصَدَّقْ مَكَانَهُ بِنَحْوٍ مِنْ ثَمَنِهِ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2360 - Hariz narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who was gifted domestic pigeons while he was in the Haram and in a state of being non-muhrim.
Imam (as) said: "If he takes any of them, he should give charity equivalent to its value."
